channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Sweden set to ban mobile phones in schools, joining trend of shelving screens for students
item.description:
Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year as part of a broad, international reversal on the use of screens in classrooms.
Since 2023, the Scandinavian country's center-rig Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Russian drone attack kills four in Ukraine's Kyiv region, officials say
item.description: A Russian drone attack on a dairy factory in a region surrounding the Ukrainian capital Kyiv has killed four people and injured seven more, Ukrainian officials said on Friday. The attack destroyed an administrative building and damaged two cars at the site, the regional governor Mykola Kalashnyk s Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Norway recovers porcelain, chandeliers from 18th century shipwreck
item.description: Archaeologists have recovered a trove of Chinese porcelain and European-made goods from a recently discovered 18th-century shipwreck off the coast of Norway, government and museum officials said on Monday. Among the goods found in the remains of the unnamed sailing ship were tightly stacked white Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Denmark's Frederiksen secures third term as prime minister
item.description: Denmark's Social Democratic leader Mette Frederiksen said on Monday she has agreed to form a centre-left coalition government, maintaining her grip on power amid a crisis in ties with US President Donald Trump over the future of Greenland. The deal to form a minority cabinet gives Frederiksen a th Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Airbus, Air France found guilty of corporate manslaughter over 2009 Rio-Paris crash
item.description: Airbus and Air France were found guilty on ‌Thursday of corporate manslaughter by a Paris appeals court over the 2009 Rio-Paris plane crash that killed 228 passengers and crew, three years after being acquitted in a lower court. Relatives of some of those who died when the Airbus A330 vanishe Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: UK health minister preparing to resign: Times
item.description: Britain's health minister Wes Streeting is preparing to resign and could quit as early as Thursday, the Times reported on Wednesday, adding that he is likely to mount a formal challenge for the party leadership. Streeting had a brief meeting with Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Wednesday ahead of t Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Sweden warns of potential jet fuel shortage
item.description: Sweden is issuing an early warning of a potential shortage of jet fuel stemming from the war in the Middle East, Energy Minister Ebba Busch said on Tuesday. "This is based on the Energy Agency's assessment," Busch told a press conference. Sweden could in a worst case scenario face rationing of av Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: EU mutual assistance clause is unambiguous: Macron
item.description: The EU's mutual assistance clause is unambiguous, French President Emmanuel Macron said on Saturday, after the bloc's leaders asked officials to prepare a blueprint for how it would work amid doubts over Washington's commitment to NATO. President Donald Trump's criticism of NATO for failing to bac Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: BBC will cut up to 2,000 jobs to reduce costs by about 10pc
item.description: The BBC said Wednesday that it plans to cut up to 2,000 jobs to save 10% of its annual budget - 500 million pounds ($677 million) - over the next two years. The layoffs announced during a call with staff are the biggest in more than a decade at the U.K. national broadcaster. "I know this creates Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskiy arrives in Jordan for next leg of Gulf tour
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy arrived in Jordan ​on Sunday as part of a ‌Gulf tour aimed at shoring up defence ties with countries in the region amid ​rising tensions over the Iran ​war. Zelenskiy is seeking support from Gulf ⁠states for Ukraine's war against Russia ​as Western mili Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: France detains two more suspects over foiled Paris Bank of America attack
item.description: French authorities have arrested two more suspects over a ​foiled attack on Bank of America’s Paris offices, the country’s anti-terrorism prosecutor’s office said ‌on Sunday. “Two further individuals were taken into police custody last night as part of the investigatio Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: London Jewish community ambulances set ablaze in antisemitic attack, PM says
item.description: Four ambulances belonging ​to a Jewish community organisation in north London were set ablaze overnight in what British Prime ‌Minister Keir Starmer called "a deeply shocking antisemitic arson attack". The London Fire Brigade, which sent six fire engines and 40 firefighters to the scene, said Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: May was the world's second-hottest on record, EU scientists say
item.description:
The world has just experienced the second-hottest May since records began, as climate change and the developing El Niño weather pattern conspired to push up average land and sea temperatures, the European Union's Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) said on Wednesday.
· The hott Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Congo says number of confirmed Ebola cases rises to nearly 600
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo on Tuesday said confirmed Ebola cases had climbed to nearly 600, raising awareness within the local population about the importance of safety measures. The outbreak of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola was announced on May 15, though officials have since said it went Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: US launches new strikes on Iran
item.description: The United States launched strikes against Iran on Tuesday after President Donald ​Trump said Tehran had shot down a US Apache helicopter in the Strait of Hormuz, deepening doubts over a potential peace deal and further straining a fragile ceasefire. The US military said on ‌X it had targeted Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Israel kills eight in strike on Lebanon; US rescues helicopter crew from Hormuz
item.description: Israel struck the historic port city of Tyre in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, killing at least eight people, the Lebanese health ministry said. On Monday, Israel and Iran halted direct attacks on each other after an appeal by US President Donald Trump for them to stop, but Tehran said it would resu Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee is unlawful, US judge rules
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump appoints ally and FHFA chief Pulte as acting US intelligence director
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Rubio to testify before Congress for the first time since the start of the Iran war
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 11 confirmed dead in Washington state chemical accident
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump to headline 250th anniversary fair opening after performers drop out
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Pentagon chief sounds 'alarm' over China's buildup, urges allies to boost defence spend
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Suspect killed during shooting near White House
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: New York Times sues Pentagon again over media access restrictions
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Mexico denies CNN report on deadly CIA operations against cartels
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump administration says its war in Iran has been ‘terminated’ before 60-day deadline
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump lifts tariffs on UK whisky to toast departing King Charles
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Prosecution of ex-FBI chief Comey over seashell post is flawed, experts say
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Indian home minister Amit Shah launches Land Port Management System
item.description: Indian Home Minister Amit Shah has launched the Land Port Management System (LPMS), a centralised digital platform to modernise operations across the country’s land ports by integrating cargo, passenger and vehicle processing on a single interface, reports bdnews24.com, citing Hindustan Times Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill at least 13 in Afghanistan, Taliban says
item.description: At least 13 people were killed, including 11 children, after Pakistan's military launched air strikes in three Afghan provinces, Afghan Taliban spokesperson Zabihullah Mujahid said on Wednesday, in a renewal of a conflict that has killed hundreds this year. At least 14 others - all of them childre Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Indian PM Modi to visit France, Slovakia from June 13-18
item.description: Indian ‌Prime ​Minister ​Narendra Modi ⁠will ​visit France ​and Slovakia ​from ​June 13 to ‌18, ⁠the Indian ​foreign ​ministry ⁠said ​on ​Tuesday. Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Thai jobs for Myanmar refugees could show way forward for Asian nations, UN says
item.description: More than 5,500 Myanmar refugees living in camps along Thailand's border have found jobs since Bangkok eased employment curbs last year, an approach that offers a regional example, a senior UN refugee official told Reuters. The step came in response to a sharp decline in global humanitarian fundin Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 4,800 sent to Bangladesh, 836 await deportation: Suvendu
item.description: Amid continued border standoffs, the West Bengal government claims to have deported nearly 4,800 “illegal immigrants” to Bangladesh, and is holding another 836 in custody pending repatriation. The Indian state’s Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari told a meeting of his Bharatiya Jana Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Delhi hotel owner helped Bangladeshis get fake Indian IDs
item.description: Lavkesh Bajaj, the proprietor of New Delhi’s Flourish Stay bed-and-breakfast, where a devastating fire claimed 21 lives, reportedly has a history of legal troubles. The 60-year-old was previously arrested by Delhi Police in 2025 for allegedly helping Bangladeshi nationals obtain forged India Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Complaint lodged against Mamata Banerjee over Bangladesh killing remarks
item.description: A complaint has been filed against former West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee, alleging that her recent public remarks critical of constitutional institutions and “linking the Union government to a political killing in Bangladesh” were “prejudicial to the nation’s sov Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: India sees 'perfect complimentarity' with Venezuela in energy trade amid Gulf crisis
item.description: India and ​Venezuela sought to boost their ties in the energy sector on Thursday with New Delhi ‌saying that Caracas sees it as a preferred partner in the sector at a time global oil supplies have been disrupted by the Middle East crisis. Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez is visit Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Malaysia bars under-16s from signing up for social media
item.description: Malaysia has begun barring those aged under 16 from registering accounts on social media platforms, its communications regulator said on Monday, as it boosts efforts to protect minors from exposure to harmful content online. The Southeast Asian nation joins a growing number of countries introducin Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Soaring prices during the Iran war jeopardize travel to tourism-dependent countries in Asia
item.description: With summer around the corner, soaring prices and other complications from the war with Iran are straining the tourism-dependent economies of countries in Southeast Asia, including Thailand and Vietnam. The region's peak tourist summer season is at risk as elevated jet fuel costs coupled with ceas Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Myanmar President visits India to boost bilateral cooperation
item.description: Myanmar President Min Aung Hlaing on Saturday began an official visit to India, marking his first trip to the neighboring country since assuming the presidency in April after an election widely criticized by opponents as a move to preserve military rule. According to Myanmar’s state-run MRTV Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 18 killed in Afghanistan road accident
item.description: At least 18 people lost their lives and 30 others sustained injuries when a truck carrying recently returned Afghan refugees overturned in eastern Afghanistan's Laghman province early Saturday, the provincial police office said in a statement. The deadly accident took place in Surkhakan region, Qa Delivered by PolitePaul service
